title: Fraction to Decimal Calculator
description: Converts fractions (including mixed numbers) to decimal equivalents with precision, uncertainty analysis, and industrial-grade rounding per ISO 80000-1 and Six Sigma standards.

inputs.label: Numerator
inputs.businessContext: The top part of the fraction (dividend). For mixed numbers, use the fractional part only.

inputs.label: Denominator
inputs.businessContext: The bottom part of the fraction (divisor). Must be non-zero.

inputs.label: Whole Number (for mixed fractions)
inputs.businessContext: Integer part of a mixed number (e.g., 3 in 3 1/4). Set to 0 for proper fractions.

inputs.label: Decimal Places (precision)
inputs.businessContext: Number of decimal places for the result. Follows ISO 80000-1 rounding rules.

inputs.label: Rounding Method
inputs.businessContext: Rounding rule per ISO 80000-1 (half_even recommended for statistical work).

inputs.label: Measurement Uncertainty (±)
inputs.businessContext: Absolute uncertainty in the numerator (e.g., from measurement tolerance). Used for Six Sigma uncertainty propagation.

rules.message: Decimal places must be between 0 and 15.

thresholds.message: Denominator has prime factors other than 2 and 5 — decimal may be repeating.
Relative uncertainty exceeds 10% — result may be unreliable.

formulas.name: Fractional Value (without whole part)
formulas.description: Simple division of numerator by denominator.

formulas.name: Mixed Number Value
formulas.description: Adds whole number part to fractional value.

formulas.name: Raw Decimal (unrounded)
formulas.description: The exact decimal representation before rounding.

formulas.name: Rounded Decimal
formulas.description: Decimal rounded to specified places using chosen method.

formulas.name: Propagated Uncertainty
formulas.description: Standard uncertainty propagation for division: delta = (uncertaintyInput / denominator).

formulas.name: Data Confidence Adjusted Decimal
formulas.description: Rounded decimal adjusted by uncertainty (lower bound). Used for conservative estimates in Lean/Six Sigma.

formulas.name: Primary Result (Final Decimal)
formulas.description: The final decimal value with applied rounding and uncertainty adjustment.

primary.label: Decimal Equivalent
primary.description: The final decimal value of the fraction, rounded and adjusted for uncertainty.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Repeating Decimal Precision Loss
hiddenLossDrivers.description: When denominator has factors other than 2 and 5, the decimal repeats infinitely, causing truncation error.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Rounding Method Bias
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Systematic bias introduced by rounding method (e.g., floor vs ceiling).

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Measurement Uncertainty
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Uncertainty in numerator propagates into the decimal result.

suggestedActions.label: Use 'half_even' rounding for statistical work
suggestedActions.description: ISO 80000-1 recommends half_even (banker's rounding) to reduce cumulative bias.

suggestedActions.label: Increase decimal places if repeating decimal detected
suggestedActions.description: For denominators with factors other than 2 and 5, use more decimal places to minimize truncation error.

suggestedActions.label: Reduce measurement uncertainty in numerator
suggestedActions.description: If propagated uncertainty exceeds acceptable tolerance, improve measurement process (Six Sigma DMAIC).

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data Confidence Adjusted Decimal
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Conservative estimate subtracting propagated uncertainty from rounded decimal.
